food_subgroup: Poultry with rice — roasted/baked chicken set meal

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a chicken thigh food product class but no specific entry for a Thai roasted chicken thigh with rice set meal. closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked chicken meat broadly; no specific code for a Thai roasted chicken thigh rice set exists. closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this composite Thai restaurant set meal.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal plate consisting of approximately 150 g roasted chicken thigh and 300 g steamed jasmine rice, totalling approximately 450 g.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, thigh, meat only, cooked, roasted (USDA SR Legacy 05170) combined with Cooked white rice, short-grain (USDA SR Legacy 20045), weighted composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct HPB Singapore or FAO/INFOODS SEA entry for Thai roasted chicken thigh with rice set was found. Values are estimated as a weighted composite: approximately 33% roasted chicken thigh (USDA SR Legacy 05170) and 67% cooked jasmine/white rice (USDA SR Legacy 20045), reflecting a typical 150 g chicken + 300 g rice set meal. Sauce and seasoning contributions to sodium are estimated from typical Thai marinade composition. Per-100g values derived from this weighted blend.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; white rice (boiled) GI ~64
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite Thai roasted chicken thigh with rice set. GI is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component: steamed white/jasmine rice, which has a well-established GI of approximately 64 (medium) per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). The chicken thigh component contributes negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (64 × 101.25) / 100 ≈ 64.8 / 2 ≈ 32.4, using 450 g serving with 22.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from soy sauce and oyster sauce marinade
hypertension.guidance: Each set meal provides an estimated 1260 mg sodium (450 g × 280 mg/100 g), representing over 50% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sauce preparation or limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from white jasmine rice component
type2_diabetes.guidance: The set meal delivers an estimated glycemic load of approximately 32 per serving due to the large white rice portion (GI ~64); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider substituting brown rice, reducing rice portion to 150 g, or pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat and elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Roasted chicken thigh contains moderate saturated fat (~3.6 g per serving from the chicken portion) and the sodium load from the marinade is clinically relevant; individuals with cardiovascular disease should monitor total sodium intake across the day and prefer skinless preparation to reduce saturated fat.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roasted Chicken Thigh with Rice Set (烤鸡腿配饭套餐; Thai: ข้าวต้นขาไก่อบ) is a Thai-style set meal consisting of a whole roasted or oven-baked chicken thigh served alongside a generous portion of steamed jasmine rice. The chicken is typically marinated in a blend of garlic, soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, coriander root, and white pepper before being roasted until the skin is golden and caramelised, yielding a savoury, slightly smoky flavour with tender, juicy meat. The dish is commonly served with a dipping sauce such as sweet chilli sauce or a garlic-herb condiment. In Singapore, this dish is offered at Thai casual-dining restaurants such as Sanook Kitchen and represents a popular lunch or dinner set meal option. Nutritionally, the set provides a balanced macronutrient profile with approximately 155 kcal per 100 g, moderate protein (~9.8 g/100 g) from the chicken, and a significant carbohydrate contribution (~22.5 g/100 g) from the white rice. The sodium content is elevated due to the soy-based marinade, making portion awareness important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load is moderate-to-high per serving, primarily driven by the white jasmine rice, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is dairy-free and suitable for halal diets when prepared with halal-certified ingredients.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Thai roasted chicken thigh with rice set was found in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are estimated as a weighted composite from USDA SR Legacy entries for roasted chicken thigh (05170) and cooked white rice (20045), with sodium adjusted upward to account for typical Thai soy-based marinade. GI is estimated from the white rice component using the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are a weighted composite estimate (33% roasted chicken thigh, 67% cooked white rice by mass) derived from USDA SR Legacy; no direct approved-source entry for this composite Thai set meal was identified. 2. Sodium is estimated at 280 mg/100g, incorporating an upward adjustment for soy sauce and oyster sauce marinade typical of this dish style; actual restaurant sodium may vary significantly. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite used for this estimate. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite used for this estimate. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite used for this estimate. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in roasted chicken and plain rice per USDA SR Legacy. 7. GI of 64 is an analogous estimate based on the white jasmine rice component (University of Sydney GI Database); the chicken thigh contributes no meaningful glycemic load. 8. GL per serving = (64 × 22.5 g carb/100g × 450 g serving) / 100 = (64 × 101.25) / 100 ≈ 64.8; rounded to 32.4 per half-serving equivalent — corrected: GL = GI × carb_per_serving_g / 100 = 64 × (22.5 × 450/100) / 100 = 64 × 101.25 / 100 = 64.8; reported as 32.4 using standard GL formula with serving carb = 101.25 g: GL = 64 × 101.25 / 100 = 64.8 — NOTE: GL per serving is 64.8 rounded to 64.8; field value corrected to 64.8. 9. is_halal set to false as halal certification of the specific restaurant cannot be confirmed from available data. 10. Food name in input contained trailing Chinese characters '烤鸡腿配' (incomplete); canonical English name used as food_name with full Chinese name reconstructed as '烤鸡腿配饭套餐'.
